ideas.intro: Ideas for projects, activities, books, games, and more for students with a high level of interest.

items.content:
[ ] (General Lit.) Create a farm scene with playdough or clay. 
[ ] (General Lit.) Tell the story to a friend or relative who has never heard it.
[ ] (Mythology) View some of the different maps that have been made about Christian/Christiana's journeys. Students may want to follow along or even create one of their own to add to their Travel Journal. (See Geography.)				
[ ] (Mythology) Act out your favorite part of the story.				
[ ] (Mythology) Tell the story to a friend or relative who has never heard it.			
[ ] (Mythology) Make a book of your illustrations and write captions for each.

[ ] (General Lit.) Start a list of words that you would use to describe Wilbur.
[ ] (General Lit.) Create a string "drawing" of one or two of the words from your word list. Glue them to paper and draw Charlotte on the paper.
[ ] (Mythology) Continue maps.				
[ ] (Mythology) Act out your favorite part of the story.				
[ ] (Mythology) Tell the story to a friend or relative who has never heard it.			
[ ] (Mythology) Make a book of your illustrations and write captions for each.

[ ] (Classic Lit.) Continue maps.				
[ ] (Classic Lit.) Act out your favorite part of the story.				
[ ] (Classic Lit.) Tell the story to a friend or relative who has never heard it.
[ ] (Classic Lit.) Make a book of your illustrations and write captions for each.
[ ] (Mythology) Tell the story to a friend or relative who has never heard it.
[ ] (Mythology) Act out your favorite part of the story.

resources.title: D'Aulaire's Book of Greek Myths
resources.author: Ingri & Edgar Parin D'Aulaire
resources.rationale: A good introduction to the characters of Greek Mythology.

resources.title: Tales from Shakespeare
resources.author: Charles and Mary Lamb
resources.rationale: An introduction to the study of Shakespeare.
